mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat on average when RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a specific threshold, it signals a tightening monetary policy stance, increasing the cost of capital for businesses and reducing the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat on average when FEDFUNDS is consistently above 5.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and strong payroll employment growth indicates a robust labor market and healthy consumer spending capacity. This fuels corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns, particularly during economic expansions.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat when UNRATE is below 5.0% and PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 0.1%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth, corporate earnings are boosted by higher output and capacity utilization. This positive supply-side dynamic leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially during economic expansions.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month average growth exceeds 0.2% during an economic expansion but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts signals a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand. This negative trend impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% over a 3-month period but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% for three consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, reduced consumer confidence, and potential recessionary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong but the Federal Funds Rate is also rising, it indicates an economy that is overheating, prompting aggressive monetary tightening that eventually stifles demand and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month average change > 0.25%
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS increases meet the trigger conditions, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive, this hypothesis is rejected.
